title: SRH create unique IPL record with unmatched Hyderabad dominance over PBKS
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad secured a historic 33-run victory over Punjab Kings in Hyderabad, marking their ninth consecutive win against PBKS at the venue. Heinrich Klaasen's 69 and Ishan Kishan's 55 powered SRH to 235/4. Despite Cooper Connolly's maiden IPL century (107*), PBKS fell short, allowing SRH to top the IPL 2026 table.

title: China should not have ... : Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang after share falls to zero in Beijing
content: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declared America a priority for advanced chip sales, stating China should not have access to the "latest and greatest" AI technology. However, he urged the US not to abandon the Chinese market entirely, arguing that global sales and increased tax revenues strengthen American economic and national security.

title: Mohsin Naqvi-led PCB rocked by billion-rupee crisis as PSL payment row explodes
content: Pakistan Cricket Board is under financial strain. It is seeking billions in unpaid dues from various entities. This includes Pakistan Super League franchises and broadcast partners. Some franchises have cleared their dues but are awaiting their revenue share. A major rights holder owes a significant amount. This situation is impacting the board's financial records and operations.

title: As Dell CEO moves company’s base to Texas; gets message from Governor Greg Abott
content: Dell Technologies is moving its legal home from Delaware to Texas. This change is set for approval at the company's annual stockholders meeting on June 25, 2026. Texas Governor Greg Abbott welcomed the tech giant, highlighting its long history in the state.

title: ‘IPL not as good as everyone thinks’: Cook’s bold claim stirs fresh controversy
content: Former England captain Alastair Cook defended his advice to Jacob Bethell regarding IPL opportunities, emphasizing the need for game time over just learning from top players.  Cook acknowledged Bethell has since played more, but reiterated that public criticism of the IPL is unlikely due to financial incentives and career implications, suggesting both viewpoints can be valid.

title: As western disturbance calms down, temperature to rise in Delhi
content: Delhi's maximum temperature is expected to rise gradually by 4-6 degrees Celsius over the next five days as the impact of a western disturbance wanes. Night temperatures are also predicted to climb, potentially reaching 27 degrees Celsius by the weekend. Meanwhile, the Air Quality Index has deteriorated to moderate and may turn poor next week.

title: 8 weeks into politics, Nitish Kumar's son Nishant Kumar may join Bihar cabinet today
content: In a significant political maneuver, Nishant Kumar, the offspring of Nitish Kumar, is poised to enter Bihar's cabinet under the leadership of CM Samrat Choudhary. This swift advancement into the realm of governance aligns with JD(U)'s call for equitable representation alongside the BJP.

title: Anthropic’s top exec reminds software engineering is dead, no manual coding at firm
content: Anthropic's Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, says software engineering is already dead inside his own company—no one writes code by hand anymore, and Claude AI instances autonomously communicate over Slack to resolve problems across teams. As Google, Meta, Amazon and Snap race to set aggressive AI coding targets, Cherny believes IDE tools like VS Code and Xcode are next to disappear—and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei's "centaur phase" warning suggests the window for human coders is closing fast.

title: 'Raja Shivaji' box office Day 6: Riteish Deshmukh starrer earns Rs 4.25 crore
content: The cinematic blockbuster 'Raja Shivaji', featuring Riteish Deshmukh in the lead, is smashing records at the box office, raking in over Rs 57 crore. With impressive collections of Rs 4.25 crore on its sixth day alone, the film has silenced critics who questioned Deshmukh's ability to embody Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj, inviting large crowds to experience his gripping portrayal. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Simone Biles 'in therapy' as Jonathan Owens eyes NFL return
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: What happened to Kyle Loftis? Questions grow after 1320 video founder’s sudden death
content: Kyle Loftis, founder of 1320Video, died Tuesday night, according to the platform’s official statement. His cause of death has not been confirmed yet. Loftis helped build one of the biggest motorsports media brands online through street racing and automotive content. Fans, racers, and creators across social media continue sharing tributes and remembering his influence on modern car culture.

title: Fact check: DNA test rumors for Dianna Russini’s sons are false parody claims
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affair rumors have only gone worse in the last few weeks as she had to step down from her successful career, sparking outrage among fans. While Mike Vrabel’s life remains unchanged, all focus is on the children that Dianna Russini and her husband, Kevin Goldschmidt, share together, as the popular coach gets dragged into it.

title: Connolly’s unbeaten ton in vain after SRH defeat PBKS by 33 runs
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad defeated Punjab Kings by 33 runs to move to the top of the IPL table after posting 235/4. Heinrich Klaasen and Ishan Kishan starred with the bat, while Punjab’s poor fielding and dropped catches proved costly. Cooper Connolly’s unbeaten century was not enough as PBKS slumped to their third straight loss and ninth consecutive defeat in Hyderabad.

title: Go, Gogoi, Gone: Why Congress' Assam defeat signals a deeper crisis
content: Gaurav Gogoi's defeat in Assam's 2026 assembly elections, despite being the Congress's chief ministerial face and a legacy leader, underscores the party's shrinking footprint. The BJP secured a massive victory, winning 102 seats, while Congress managed only 19. This outcome highlights a deeper political churn, impacting legacy, identity, and strategy.

title: Mediation fails in ‘Rang De Lal’ row; Delhi HC to hear case May 8
content: The copyright dispute between Trimurti Films and the makers of Dhurandhar 2 over the song ‘Rang De Lal (Oye Oye)’ has taken a new turn. The Delhi High Court was informed that mediation between both sides has failed. Justice Tushar Rao Gedela has now listed the matter for hearing on May 8.

title: How Middle East turmoil opens 3 critical supply chain opportunities for India
content: India’s macro exposure to the Middle East continues to be transmitted primarily through energy and critical input prices, reflecting structurally high dependence on imported crude and natural gas.

title: Amanpreet, ex-teammate of Kohli, passes away at 36; Yuvraj pays tribute
content: Cricketing circles mourn the loss of Amanpreet Singh Gill, a former Punjab fast bowler and Kings XI Punjab player. He passed away at 36. Yuvraj Singh remembered him as a quiet, hardworking cricketer. Gill also served on Punjab's senior selection committee. His contributions to the game and his presence are deeply missed. The cause of death remains undisclosed.

title: India, Vietnam elevate strategic ties, set $25 billion trade target by 2030
content: India and Vietnam have agreed to elevate their relationship to an Enhanced Comprehensive Strategic Partnership, setting an ambitious trade target of $25 billion by 2030. The pact includes expanded cooperation in defense, technology, and digital services, alongside efforts to boost agricultural trade and supply chain resilience.

title: Palaash Muchhal’s lawyer calls Atrocities Act case a publicity stunt
content: Music composer and filmmaker Palaash Muchhal has landed in a legal controversy after social worker and Smriti Mandhana's friend Vidnyan Mane filed a case against him in Sangli, Maharashtra, alleging cheating and invoking the Atrocities Act. However, Muchhal's legal representative has dismissed the allegations, calling them a pressure tactic.

title: Vinesh Phogat’s Asian Games 2026 dream crushed as WFI bars her from trials
content: The Wrestling Federation of India announced new eligibility rules for the 2026 Asian Games trials, making Vinesh Phogat ineligible as only recent national and federation medal winners can participate. The returning wrestler is also facing an ITA warning for missing a doping test in December 2025. It counts as her first “whereabouts failure” but is not a doping violation.

title: Rodney Harrison backs Mike Vrabel to lead Patriots locker room amid controversy
content: Former New England Patriots star safety Rodney Harrison backs Mike Vrabel amidst Dianna Russini controversy. He predicts the locker room will rally around the coach. Harrison believes players’ shared struggles will foster empathy and forgiveness. It will potentially strengthen team bonds. Fellow analyst Devin McCourty hopes Vrabel can navigate the situation until the season begins. He emphasized that winning will help the team move past the issue as people usually forget such scandals.

title: Hardik Pandya set to link up with MI; SKY doubtful for RCB game
content: Mumbai Indians captain Hardik Pandya is likely to join the squad in Raipur on Thursday for their Sunday fixture against the Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The all-rounder didn't travel with the first batch of players on Wednesday and will link up with the squad on Thursday.

title: Connor McDavid unwinds at Amangiri with Lauren Kyle after exit
content: After a loss to the Anaheim Ducks last night, Connor McDavid and Lauren Kyle are taking some time off to enjoy the pampering at Amangiri during their offseason. In the latest NHL news roundup, the blog catches up on his activities during the bye weeks, and they discuss the team’s next move.

title: NFL rumours: Browns may trade Shedeur Sanders to Steelers as Rodgers exit looms
content: Speculation is extensively mounting that the Cleveland Browns may favor Deshaun Watson as their 2026 season’s starting quarterback. This could potentially open the door for Shedeur Sanders’ departure. FanSided suggests a trade to the Pittsburgh Steelers. They are seeking a long-term solution beyond veteran quarterback Aaron Rodgers. This move could offer Shedeur Sanders a prime opportunity to develop with a divisional rival.

title: Vikings eye ex-Ravens $5M WR to fix offense after tough season
content: The Minnesota Vikings have had a pretty brutal run on the field in the last season after a pretty impressive performance in the 2024 season, sparking concern among fans. With 14 wins and 3 losses in the 2024 season, the Minnesota Vikings ended up recording just 9 wins and 8 losses in the previous season. Ahead of the new season, the team remains focused on fixing the roster.

title: Strait of Hormuz technically open, but not operating: National shipowners' body head
content: Commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz faces immense risks despite remaining technically open, with Indian vessels experiencing significant disruptions and safety concerns.  Seafarers exhibit hesitation due to past incidents, impacting normal traffic flow and leading to increased operational costs for shipowners.

title: 65% of newly elected Bengal MLAs face criminal cases; 61% are ‘crorepatis’: ADR
content: A party-wise analysis showed that 152 of 206 winning candidates (74%) from the Bharatiya Janata Party have declared criminal cases, followed by 34 out of 80 (43%) from the All India Trinamool Congress

title: T.N. Governor asks TVK chief Vijay to produce proof of support of majority MLAs; Congress says ‘yes’ but AIADMK ‘no’
content: The TVK has emerged as the single largest party in Tamil Nadu Assembly, winning 108 seats in the 234-member House; DMK’s allies CPI(M), CPI, and VCK, which have won two seats each, are yet to decide on support; IUML has refused backing

title: Slain Chandranath Rath: From air force serviceman to Suvendu’s trusted troubleshooter
content: There had even been speculation that Rath could be entrusted with a larger administrative responsibility if Adhikari assumed a bigger role in government after the Assembly polls

title: NC, PDP in war of words over Rajya Sabha polls, closure of seminary, ‘sidelining’ Urdu
content: “Those who aided the BJP in the Rajya Sabha polls were exposed through Right To Information (RTI). No one in my party will act against the organisation,” said J&K Chief Minister Omar Abdullah

title: In topsy-turvy Haryana politics, Sonipat latest amphitheatre for BJP-Congress fight
content: Locked in a prestige battle with Congress’s Kamal Dewan, Outgoing Sonipat Mayor Rajiv Jain said he was relying on his public image and the work done during his nine-month stint as mayor after winning the bypoll in March last year

title: CEC directs West Bengal officials to ensure zero post-poll violence
content: This directive came amid concerns over law and order in parts of the State, with the Election Commission closely monitoring the situation

title: Suvendu Adhikari’s personal assistant shot dead
content: Suvendu Adhikari’s personal assistant Chandranath Rath was shot three times by unidentified gunmen in Madhyamgram area of the North 24 Parganas district

title: Suvendu aide among 3 killed as post-poll violence rocks Bengal
content: Zero tolerance for violence, says CEC Gyanesh Kumar; State BJP leadership urges workers to remain calm; DGP tells aggrieved parties to approach police and not break the law

title: Mumbai police detain man after threat call to Taj, Trident hotels
content: The police traced the call to a person in Sion; the individual has been detained for questioning and authorities are trying to establish why the call was made

title: Rise in cybercrime but overall crime rate dropped in 2024, says latest NCRB report
content: Deaths due to drug overdose saw a 50% increase in 2024 compared with the previous year, with Tamil Nadu recording most fatalities; 5,194 cases of offences ‘against the state’ registered, a rise of over 6% from the previous year

title: Dutch frigate arrives in Kochi as part of efforts to strengthen maritime cooperation
content: The Dutch delegation called on Rear Admiral Prakash Gopalan; discussions focused on enhancing cooperation in the maritime domain

title: Rubio to visit India for bilateral talks; Quad Foreign Ministers’ meeting May-end
content: Rubio will hold talks with Jaishankar and Ajit Doval, and call on Prime Minister Modi, in discussions that are expected to set the course for India-U.S. ties after several ups and downs over tariffs and sanctions

title: CBI files charge sheet in builder-bank nexus case
content: The agency alleged a conspiracy by public servants and other private persons to induce homebuyers and investors through false assurances and fraudulent representations

title: Preparation on for Bihar Cabinet expansion amid  Nishant Kumar’s induction buzz
content: Senior JD(U) leaders hold series of meetings in Patna, including at the residence of the party’s national working president, Sanjay Jha, leading to speculation over possible political debut of Nishant Kumar

title: Delhi to host 2026 Commonwealth Table Tennis Championship
content: The championship is being organised from July 27 to August 2, jointly by the Delhi government and the Table Tennis Federation of India

title: Congress leader Varsha Gaikwad writes to Maharashtra Governor, seeks immediate enactment of Shakti Bill
content: The request comes amid massive protests over the alleged rape and murder of a four-year-old girl at Nasrapur village in Pune district

title: In a first, world’s smallest wild cat snapped with kitten in Faridabad; confirms breeding outside protected areas, say researchers
content: The adult and its dependent young were spotted during routine field surveys by a team of researchers monitoring mammalian diversity

title: The roller-coaster relationship between the DMK and Congress finally derails
content: Whenever the two parties engaged in tough bargaining over seat-sharing—first in 2011, when the Congress insisted on being allotted 90 seats and eventually got over 60, and again in 2026, when negotiations reached a stage of brinkmanship—the alliance ended up losing elections

title: Online process to join arts and science colleges begins today
content: Those who are unable to apply online independently could submit their applications through Admission Facilitation Centres established in all government Arts and Science colleges

title: Nearly 50 died on railway tracks Between Alappuzha and Thrissur this year
content: Of the 48 deaths this year, 20 took place on tracks between Kalamassery and Irinjalakuda, while 17 occurred on lines connecting Irinjalakuda with Vallathol Nagar

title: Overseas scholarship forAdi Dravidar, tribal students
content: Candidates should have secured admission to a Master’s or a PhD programme at a foreign university ranked within the top 1000 as per QS World University Rankings, and their annual family income should not exceed ₹12 lakh

title: Tamil Nadu government formation: DMK rejects overtures from AIADMK, sources say
content: The DMK was firm on sitting in the Opposition and not interested in going against the people’s mandate, sources said.

title: Gram Sabhas that gave nod to Nicobar project did not have mandatory 50% quorum
content: Rules mandate that a quorum at a Gram Sabha is achieved only if 50% of the adult population is in attendance, but figures submitted in Calcutta High Court show attendance was between 1% to 12% of the total population of the villages

title: Malkajgiri CP goes undercover at midnight to assess women’s safety
content: Instead of booking criminal cases, police counsel youth, warning them about legal consequences of harassment and stressing the importance of respecting women in public spaces

title: Vellore Corporation begins removal of dumped wastes along Palar river
content: The landfill site along the river spreads over two acres where household and commercial garbage from Zone - I and Zone - 2 of the civic body are dumped

title: Two persons arrested for illegal sand mining in Ponnai riverbed in Ranipet
content: Initial inquiry revealed that illegally mined sand had been transported to private contractors in the district

title: Vietnam major pillar of India’s Act East Policy, says Modi
content: India hosts General Secretary of Communist Party of Vietnam’s Central Committee To Lam, who is also the current Vietnamese President.

title: Rajya Sabha Opposition benches set to see many changes after Assembly poll results
content: The TVK may make its debut in the RS this summer, while the Left will lose members by April 2027, the DMK by June 2028, and the TMC by August 2029; the Congress will benefit at the cost of the Left

title: India, Algeria hold first joint defence commission meet
content: Both sides signed the Rules of Procedure to oversee the implementation of India-Algeria defence  cooperation, which will guide future joint commission engagements and streamline collaboration mechanisms between the two countries

title: Kerala elections: G. Sudhakaran dismisses talks of joining Congress, blames CPI(M)’s ‘ideological drift’ for its poll rout
content: ‘I am not exploring any broader platform or planning to join any political party. I will remain an independent MLA. This allows me to raise issues freely and intervene in matters that benefit society,’ says Sudhakaran

title: CJI was only meant to have a say in CEC/EC appointments till Parliament brought a law: Supreme Court
content: A nine-judge constitution bench headed by Chief Justice Surya Kant is currently hearing petitions regarding discrimination against women at religious sites, including the Sabarimala temple in Kerala, as well as the scope of religious freedom across various faiths

title: North Kerala districts could get fair representation in UDF Cabinet
content: Forty-five of the UDF’s 102 MLA-designates are from Kasaragod, Wayanad, Kannur, Kozhikode, Malappuram and Palakkad districts

title: Hotels down shutters in Kochi, people affected
content: Hotel owners shut outlets and stage protests outside petroleum company offices and Central government institutions; All Kerala Caterers Association to stage a State-wide kitchen-closure strike against the LPG price hike

title: Sabarimala hearing: SC questions maintainability of PILs on Dawoodi Bohra excommunication
content: Petitioners says their writ pleas were not ‘lightly drafted’, point to contradictory stands taken by the apex court on the Places of Worship Act

title: Efforts on to integrate border populations into national mainstream: J&K L-G
content: On the eve of first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, Manoj Sinha says the administration is resolute to elevate the border settlements from the periphery of neglect to the forefront of national progress

title: Voices of dissent emerge in Trinamool; leaders blame IPAC, ticket choices
content: Trinamool’s outgoing Rajganj MLA Khageswar Roy blames the IPAC for the party’s defeat; Minister of State for Sports and Youth Affairs Manoj Tiwary quits party alleging corruption

title: Vivek Ramaswamy on track to become Ohio governor with Trump backing
content: vivek Ramaswamy won in Ohio's gubernatorial primary on tuesday . he became a prominent public figure during the state's response to the Covid-19 pandemic . but the result lies less in the general election matchup and more in what it signals about the ideological trajectory of the Republican Party .

title: Tamil Nadu results: Vijay’s oath-taking delayed as he struggles to show govt majority
content: televisionK sources said the governor insisted on letters of support from an adequate number of MLAs before deciding on the swearing-in date . the ceremony, planned for Thursday, was put on hold . in the forenoon, Congress leaders called on Vijay at the TVK headquarters in Panaiyur . DMK and AIADMK were in conversation on the situation .

title: Bengal bloodshed: Suvendu’s PA killed in car near Kolkata
content: motorbike-borne assailants fatally shot BJP's BJP’s aide . aide, Buddhadeb Bera, critically injured in their car in a Madhyamgram lane . the killing capped a day of post-poll political violence in parts of the state .

title: Power play in Kerala: Congress to finalise CM by Sunday
content: Rajiv G is a senior assistant editor with the Times of India . he began his journey in 1998 with the Indian Express in Coimbatore before moving on to Deccan Chronicle in Hyderabad . since joining the Times in 2011, he has been at the forefront of reporting on the health sector .

title: Clashes, firing, vandalism rock West Bengal, BJP calls for calm
content: post-election flareups across Bengal triggered clashes, firing and vandalism . most serious confrontation came from Sandeshkhali in north 24 Parganas district . three policemen and two CRPF personnel were wounded by gunfire .

title: NCRB: Fake notes with Rs 55 crore face value confiscated in 2024
content: Raj Shekhar Jha is a journalist for the Times of India with over a decade of experience in reporting on national security, terrorism, crime and prisons.

title: Suru River tragedy: Pakistan hands over body of Ladakh minor after 46 days
content: a nine-year-old boy from Kargil drowned in suru river and was swept into Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) authorities handed over his remains to Indian officials at Teetwal-Keran crossing in north Kashmir’s Kupwara district .

title: 'Watch without disturbing for 6 months': MK Stalin’s message as TVK prepares to form govt
content: outgoing CM MK Stalin said DMK didn't want a constitutional crisis or another election soon . he said the new govt should continue with the free breakfast scheme for schoolchildren . but he also said it would be difficult to keep that promise .

title: West Bengal: 9 of 80 TMC MLAs give Mamata’s 1st post-election meet a miss
content: Tamaghna Banerjee, a reporter from Kolkata, covers crime, aviation, human rights and politics. He has a keen interest in human interest and rural reporting. He has done his postgraduation in journalism and mass communication. He has a total of 14 years in journalism. Read More

title: Arrest those indulging in violence: CEC Gyanesh Kumar
content: DMs and SPs are directed to ensure continuous patrolling in sensitive areas . authorities are maintaining strict vigil in all districts in coordination with the central forces . two deaths and widespread vandalism raised concerns about law and order in the state .

title: West Bengal CM oath: PM Modi, Amit Shah to attend BJP’s first swearing-in on Saturday
content: Rohit Khanna covers sectors like government finance, economy, industry and issues related to financial crime.

title: Cash recovery row: Justice Yashwant Varma continues as HC judge despite resignation
content: yashwant Varma's name figures at serial number four of the list of judges in HC official portal . he is preceded by justices M C Tripathi, Arindam Sinha and Ranjan Roy . this is because he has not yet accepted his resignation from the post of HC judge .

title: One year of Operation Sindoor: How India recalibrated its national security approach
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. title: From Air Force Veteran To Suvendu Adhikari's Aide: Who Was Chandranath Rath?
content: Chandranath Rath, the close aide of BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari who was shot dead in West Bengal's North 24 Parganas district, had travelled an unusual path — from serving in the Indian Air Force to becoming one of the opposition leader's most trusted political associates.

title: US President Trump's Renewed Trade War With China
content: US President Donald Trump is set to meet Chinese President Xi Jinping in May on his first visit to China in eight years.

title: Trump Official Lutnick Testifies On Epstein Island Lunch
content: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick told members of a congressional committee in private testimony on Wednesday that he couldn't recall why he and his family had lunch on Jeffrey Epstein's private island, members said.

title: Suvendu Adhikari's Aide Murdered: What Top Cop Said
content: West Bengal Police on Thursday seized a vehicle in connection with the probe into the alleged murder of Chandra, personal assistant to West Bengal Leader of Opposition Suvendu Adhikari.

title: "India Forgives Nothing": Air Force Posts Video On 1 Year Of Op Sindoor
content: Operation Sindoor Anniversary: At 1.05 am on Thursday, the IAF in a post on X shared a video with incredibly sharp footage of some moments from Operation Sindoor.

title: "Cold-Blooded Murder": Suvendu Adhikari On His Aide's Killing
content: In a shocking development within 48 hours of the assembly poll verdict, Rath, Adhikari's executive assistant, was shot dead in a brazen attack at Madhyamgram on Wednesday night, triggering sharp political reactions and escalating tension in the area.

title: Trump Says Iran Deal "Very Possible" Following Latest Talks
content: US President Donald Trump on Wednesday told reporters that the U.S. has had very good talks with Iran in the last 24 hours.

title: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran, China Envoys Meet As Trump Pauses US Effort In Hormuz
content: US-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: The attacks shattered a period of relative calm in the Middle East since the ceasefire took effect on April 8.

title: China Rolls Out J-35AE Stealth Jet As F-35 Rival, Pak May Be First Buyer
content: The aircraft carried the branding of the Aviation Industry Corporation of China instead of air force markings, signalling its export role.

title: 75-Year-Old "Lawyer" Arrested For Sexually Assaulting Minor In Madhya Pradesh
content: In a shocking case of crime that has emerged from one of the most upscale colonies under the jurisdiction of Habibganj Police Station in Bhopal, a 75-year-old "lawyer" has been arrested for sexually assaulting a five-year-old girl.

title: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Highlights: Vijay Oath-Taking Set To Be Delayed, AIADMK MLAs Moved To Resort: Sources
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: The TVK emerged as the single-largest party with 108 seats in the 234-member assembly, falling short of the majority mark of 118.

content: Bengal Election Results 2026: The BJP on Monday won 208 of the state's 294 seats, while the Trinamool was reduced to 79.

content: The bench also said that its judgment, which allows the Chief Justice of India to be on the selection panel, was an arrangement made in the absence of a law.

title: 200 Cases, 433 Arrested Over Post-Poll Violence In Bengal: Cops
content: In addition to the 433 arrests made for direct involvement in cases of post-poll violence, the DGP said that 1,100 persons have so far been placed under preventive detention.

content: This is a clear case - Vijay's TVK is the number one party by a mile in a hung verdict and needs to have the chance to prove its numbers on the floor of the house

content: The boy, identified as Shiba Pradhan, had ventured into the Kanipada forest with his uncle to collect honey.

title: Dhaka raises concern over illegal migrant expulsions after BJP's win in Bengal, Assam polls
content: 'pushbacks' have been a thorny issue in Indo-Bangladesh ties . the issue is seen by many in Bangladesh as a hurdle to ties, foreign secretary says . 'we have weathered a testing time,' says foreign secretary .

title: INDIA bloc under strain: Congress breaks 11-year DMK partnership, shifts support to TVK
content: DMK's rout in Tamil Nadu deepens fissures in INDIA bloc . aICC in-charge Girish Chodankar met televisionK chief C Joseph Vijay . the switch redraws the state's political map and blunts the pitch of a united front against BJP .

title: Suvendu Adhikari calls killing of aide Chandranath Rath a 'pre-planned murder'
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
